summary: Russian officials claim Ukraine is deploying American humanoid robots (Phantom MK-1) in combat, sparking alarm over the shift from drone warfare to autonomous "embodied AI" systems. On the ground, both sides continue intense FPV drone operations: Ukrainian forces are targeting Russian positions with coordinated strikes, including multi-drone attacks and efforts to dismount troops from vehicles, while Russian units like "Rubikon" report 110 enemy kills in a single day across multiple fronts. Russian defenses are adapting, with reports of log-periodic antennas on Ukrainian drones improving range and jamming resistance, and units like "Elka" and "Dnipro" conducting kinetic and electronic countermeasures, tallying dozens of intercepted UAVs. Meanwhile, unconventional tactics emerge, such as a Russian soldier attempting to charge an FPV drone with a bayonet—an incident described as a first in warfare—and Iranian-backed militias launching fiber-optic FPV strikes on U.S. bases in Iraq.
